overview: Activity on July 17-18 spanned JIT improvements across arm64 and parisc architectures, arena memory management enhancements, and verifier correctness fixes. Puranjay Mohan was particularly active, contributing patches to inline numeric iterator kfuncs, fix arm64 indirect trampolines, and optimize cast_user code generation.

patches.title: bpf, arm64: Fix stack-passed arguments for indirect trampolines
patches.author: Puranjay Mohan
patches.summary: Fixes incorrect handling of stack-passed arguments in the arm64 BPF JIT when generating indirect trampolines. Indirect trampolines are used for BPF programs that attach to kernel functions, and passing arguments via the stack rather than registers requires special care to preserve the calling convention. This bug could cause incorrect argument values to be seen by attached BPF programs or by the wrapped kernel function.

patches.title: bpf, arm64: Optimize cast_user code generation
patches.author: Puranjay Mohan
patches.summary: Improves the efficiency of code generated by the arm64 JIT for the cast_user operation, which is used when BPF programs access user-space memory addresses. The optimization reduces the number of instructions emitted for this common pattern, improving performance of BPF programs that perform user-space pointer manipulation.

patches.title: bpf: Inline bpf_iter_num_new() kfunc
patches.author: Puranjay Mohan
patches.summary: Part of a four-patch series (v2) to inline the numeric open-coded iterator kfuncs (bpf_iter_num_new, bpf_iter_num_next, bpf_iter_num_destroy) directly into the BPF JIT rather than emitting calls to them. Open-coded iterators, used with the bpf_for() macro, are a core ergonomics feature; inlining their kfuncs eliminates function call overhead and enables better code generation for loop-heavy BPF programs.

patches.title: bpf: Reject arena frees below the arena base
patches.author: Yiyang Chen
patches.summary: Adds a verifier check to reject BPF programs that attempt to free memory at addresses below the arena base pointer. BPF arenas are shared-memory regions accessible from both BPF programs and user space; freeing below-base addresses would corrupt arena metadata or adjacent memory. This is a second-version revision that also adds a selftest covering the invalid-free scenario.

patches.title: bpf: Add memory usage for arena
patches.author: Jiayuan Chen
patches.summary: Adds memory usage accounting to BPF arena maps, allowing the kernel to track how much physical memory is backing a given arena. This is part of a four-patch series that also refactors how arenas pass page-table callbacks (replacing scratch_page with the arena pointer) and adds selftests. Better accounting enables future memory-pressure handling and exposes useful introspection for operators.

patches.title: bpf: Check byte type when validating helper stack reads
patches.author: Yiyang Chen
patches.summary: Fixes a verifier gap where helper functions reading from the BPF stack did not fully validate the type of each byte being read. Spilled registers on the stack can have sibling bytes that are in an invalid state, and reading through a helper without checking could allow type-unsafe data to escape into kernel helpers. The accompanying selftest covers the invalid-spill-sibling scenario.

patches.title: bpf, parisc: Add support for BPF_SDIV and BPF_SMOD in the parisc64 JIT
patches.author: Nicholas Dudar
patches.summary: Extends the parisc64 BPF JIT to emit native signed division and signed modulo instructions for the BPF_SDIV and BPF_SMOD opcodes. Previously these operations would fall back to interpretation on parisc. Part of a two-patch series that also adds the same support to the parisc32 JIT, bringing parisc into parity with other JIT-capable architectures for signed integer arithmetic.
